title: Floral Polish Cherry Pie
description: A fragrant, vegan cherry pie with a lavender‑rose water infused filling that oozes gooey cherry goodness—perfect for adventurous dessert lovers.

ingredients.name: cold water
ingredients.notes: use ice water for a firmer crust

instructions: In a large bowl, mix flour, salt, and sugar. Add chilled butter cubes; use a pastry blender or fingertips to cut butter into the flour until mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
Add cold water, 1 tablespoon at a time, tossing with a fork until dough just comes together; form into a disk, wrap in plastic, and chill 30 minutes.

Meanwhile, in a saucepan over medium heat, combine cherry jam, corn­starch, lemon zest, vanilla, and 1 tablespoon of rose water. Stir until smooth and thickened, about 3–4 minutes. Remove from heat and stir in lavender buds and remaining rose water. Let mixture cool to room temperature while dough chills.
On a lightly floured surface, roll chilled dough to a 12‑inch circle. Place on a parchment‑lined 9‑inch pie plate, trim edges, and crimp the rim with a fork or your fingers.
Pour cooled cherry filling into the crust; scatter chocolate chips evenly over the surface for added gooeyness.
Bake for 45–50 minutes until the crust is golden and the filling is bubbling. If the edges brown too quickly, cover with foil for the last 10 minutes.
Remove pie from oven and let it cool on a rack for 15 minutes to set the filling.
Slice and serve warm or at room temperature. Pair with a drizzle of coconut cream for extra indulgence. Enjoy the fragrant floral notes that transport you to a Polish garden!
